questions.question: In a Time-Sharing Environment, what is the primary role of the central computer regarding resources?
options: A) It solely provides individual users with personal computation devices.
B) It manages all shared resources, shared data, and printing for many connected users.
C) Its main function is to seamlessly integrate computing functions between different servers globally.
D) It only stores data, with all processing handled by individual user workstations.
questions.correct_answer: B) It manages all shared resources, shared data, and printing for many connected users.

questions.question: What unique feature of Assembly Language led to its alternative name 'Symbolic Programming Language'?
options: A) Its ability to directly execute instructions in binary digits (0s and 1s).
B) Its use of English-like words and mathematical symbols in a one-to-many translation.
C) Its representation of operation codes and operands using alphanumeric mnemonic codes.
D) Its inherent portability across different types of computer hardware.
questions.correct_answer: C) Its representation of operation codes and operands using alphanumeric mnemonic codes.

questions.question: Which C operator is described as a unary operator that returns the length in bytes of the specified variable?
options: A) The comma operator (,)
B) The logical NOT operator (!)
C) The sizeof() operator
D) The bitwise AND operator (&)

questions.question: Which C storage class has a default initial value of zero and its life persists between different function calls, despite being local in scope?
options: A) auto
B) register
C) static
D) extern

questions.question: What is the primary difference in memory allocation between a struct and a union in C?
options: A) struct allocates memory at runtime, while union allocates at compile time.
B) All members of a struct share a single memory location, while union members each have their own.
C) struct members each have their own storage location, while all members of a union use a single shared memory location.
D) union can only store homogeneous data types, whereas struct can store heterogeneous types.
questions.correct_answer: C) struct members each have their own storage location, while all members of a union use a single shared memory location.
